preFix.title: Carrot and ginger soup
preFix.subtitle: Warming, bright, and sharply spiced
preFix.excerpt: Carrots blended with fresh ginger, a little orange zest, and vegetable stock into a vivid orange soup with a clean, warming heat. One of the most reliably good weekday soups: quick, cheap, and better than it sounds.
preFix.sourceNotes: Carrot and ginger soup is a British weekday staple with no single origin. The combination of carrot with fresh ginger became a soup fixture in British home cooking from the 1980s onwards as fresh ginger became widely available.

content.text: Carrot and ginger soup works because carrots are naturally sweet and ginger is naturally sharp ,  each moderates the other.

is essential here: the dried ground version lacks the aromatic brightness that makes this soup stand out from a plain carrot soup. Orange zest amplifies the sweetness of the carrot without adding liquid. The result is a warming soup that does not feel heavy.

Melt {{unsalted-butter}} of butter in a large saucepan over a medium-low heat. Add the {{onion}} onion and cook for 6 minutes until soft. Add the {{garlic}} of garlic and {{ginger-fresh}} of grated ginger and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

Add the {{carrot}} of chopped carrots and the zest of {{orange}} of orange. Stir to combine. Pour in {{stock-vegetable}} of hot stock. Bring to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes until the carrots are very tender.
Blend until very smooth. Taste and season with salt. Thin with a little more stock if needed.
Ladle into bowls. Add a spoonful of crème fraîche to each bowl if using.

items.fix: Fresh ginger should feel firm and smell strongly when grated. If the piece you have seems dry, use more. A little extra ginger grated raw into the finished bowl gives a stronger, fresher kick.
items.cause: Old ginger with dried-out flesh, or not enough was added.

items.fix: A squeeze of fresh orange juice or a splash of lemon juice added after blending cuts sweetness. The ginger should balance it but more may be needed.
items.cause: Some carrots are very sweet, especially in autumn when their sugars have concentrated.

items.fix: Blend for at least 60 seconds with a stick blender. For a finer result, use a jug blender or pass the soup through a sieve.
items.cause: Carrots have fibrous strands that a stick blender can miss.

items.fix: Add extra stock or water after blending until the soup flows easily from a ladle. A thick, almost porridge-like consistency is too far.
items.cause: Carrots produce a naturally thick purée.

content.text: Carrot and ginger soup has no specific cultural origin. It is a product of the British 1980s kitchen, when fresh ginger became available in British supermarkets and cooks began pairing it with the carrots that were already a soup staple. Before that, carrot soup tended to be plain or spiced with coriander powder. The ginger version became one of the most-made homemade soups in Britain: reliable, cheap, and fast. It is the kind of recipe that appears handwritten in family notebooks rather than in cookbooks.

initialFindings.message: tricolon: tricolon (three parallel items) — two adjectives almost always beats three
